title: AgentHandoff
description: Cross-model agent handoff: sparse state deltas plus pointers, never payloads.
$comment: Compatibility policy: schema_version is MAJOR.MINOR. A handoff is compatible with a running pigeon iff it has the SAME major and a minor <= the reader's minor (minor bumps are additive, so the current schema is a superset of older minors). A newer minor or a different major is rejected on receipt; carry an older handoff forward with `pigeon migrate`. The gate is enforced in pigeon.handoff.validate_handoff; the authoritative prose lives in that module.

sid.description: Session id grouping a chain of handoffs.

doing.description: The single step the receiver should take next.

artifacts.description: Pointers (repo://, file://, s3://, or a path) — resolved on demand, never inlined.

salvaged_upstream.description: Task IDs of upstream salvaged tasks whose diff this handoff carries (advisory; downstream reviews materialized-but-unverified work).

derived.description: Reasoning the receiver cannot cheaply re-derive from the pointed-to artifacts (added in 1.2). PAYLOAD for hard-won reasoning — approaches ruled out, a constraint discovered through failed experiments, the rationale and intended next action — never for re-derivable facts (point at code for those). Caps bound worst-case residue to ~900-1100 tokens.

ruled_out.description: Approaches tried and abandoned: what, and why it failed (so the receiver doesn't re-walk the dead end).

constraint_found.description: Non-obvious constraints discovered (one line each) that the final artifacts do not make visible.

next_action.description: The single intended next action the sender would take.

open_questions.description: Unresolved questions the receiver should weigh.

rationale.description: Why the chosen approach over the alternatives — the decision residue.

rag.description: Optional retrieval hint for the receiver.

crew.description: Deterministic staffing for the receiving agent: which skills to load and which subagents to dispatch. Names resolve against the shared knowledge tree (playbooks).

skills.description: Skill/playbook names the agent itself must load.

subagents.description: Roster the agent must dispatch via its own subagent mechanism.

context_ref.description: Logical pointer into the shared store, e.g. "manifest@HEAD".
